nodes.name: Google Drive Trigger
nodes.notes: Ingestion entry (§154). File binary flows to Init.

parameters.query: SELECT domain, key, value FROM configuration.configuration;

nodes.name: Load Config Cache
nodes.notes: R-08 runtime config. Bind CRIE Postgres/Supabase credential.

nodes.name: Load Provider Records
nodes.notes: R-08 provider records. Bind CRIE Postgres/Supabase credential.

nodes.name: Download File
nodes.notes: Downloads the file the trigger reported. fileId from Google Drive Trigger. Binary -> field 'data' (Put Output File in Field). Bind the same Google Drive OAuth credential as the trigger. Google-native docs are exported to PDF so Azure DI (SW-005) can OCR them.

nodes.name: Init (SW-001/002)
nodes.notes: SW-001 correlationId + SW-002 sha256 (pure).

parameters.query: SELECT CASE WHEN COALESCE((SELECT value::text FROM configuration.configuration WHERE domain='development' AND key='development.duplicate_detection_bypass' LIMIT 1), 'false') = 'true' THEN NULL ELSE (SELECT id FROM repository.documents WHERE sha256 = '{{$json.sha256}}' LIMIT 1) END AS "dupDocumentId", '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS "sha256", '{{$json.filename}}'     AS "filename", '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS "source";

nodes.name: SW-003 Duplicate Check
nodes.notes: Dev-mode bypass: when configuration.configuration has domain='development', key='development.duplicate_detection_bypass', value=true (jsonb), dupDocumentId is forced NULL so the pipeline reprocesses without repo writes blocking it. Absent/false -> normal SHA-256 duplicate lookup (production behavior). Always returns one row.

nodes.name: Emit DUPLICATE
nodes.notes: §154 Duplicate → Stop.

parameters.query: INSERT INTO repository.documents (sha256, filename, status, authority) VALUES ('{{$json.sha256}}','{{$json.filename}}','REGISTERED','Unspecified') ON CONFLICT (sha256) DO UPDATE SET filename = EXCLUDED.filename RETURNING id AS "documentId", '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.sha256}}'        AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'      AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'      AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'        AS source;

nodes.name: SW-004 Register Document
nodes.notes: Registers the document and ALWAYS returns its id. Uses ON CONFLICT (sha256) DO UPDATE — not DO NOTHING and not a conditional INSERT — because DO UPDATE always produces a row, so RETURNING can never be empty. Re-registering an existing sha256 returns the EXISTING id (idempotent). Replaces a CTE whose INSERT was guarded by NOT (bypass AND (SELECT id IS NOT NULL FROM existing)): for a NEW document that scalar subquery over an empty CTE returns NULL (not false), so the predicate collapsed to NULL, the INSERT was silently skipped, and COALESCE(NULL, NULL) produced documentId=null while the node reported success. Duplicate SUPPRESSION is SW-003's job (node-dupcheck -> Duplicate?), which already runs upstream; SW-004 must only register and return an id.

nodes.name: Checkpoint Upload/OCR-pending
nodes.notes: R-18 checkpoints.

nodes.name: SW-005 Azure OCR
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-005 Azure OCR' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-005). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

parameters.query: UPDATE repository.documents SET status='HUMAN_REVIEW' WHERE id='{{$json.documentId}}'; INSERT INTO monitoring.processing_history (document_id,stage,status,correlation_id) VALUES ('{{$json.documentId}}','OCR','FAILED','{{$json.correlationId}}'); SELECT '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: OCR→HUMAN_REVIEW
nodes.notes: §154 OCR fail → HUMAN_REVIEW.

nodes.name: SW-006 OCR Validate
nodes.notes: OCR validation is PARAGRAPH-FIRST against the normalized model. A DOCX legitimately has zero pages, so validity is judged on paragraphs (the universal unit). Page confidence is checked only when the format paginates natively.

nodes.name: SW-007 LLM Metadata
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-007 LLM Metadata' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-007). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

nodes.name: SW-008 LLM Knowledge
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-008 LLM Knowledge' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-008). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

parameters.query: UPDATE repository.documents SET status='HUMAN_REVIEW' WHERE id='{{$json.documentId}}'; INSERT INTO monitoring.processing_history (document_id,stage,status,correlation_id) VALUES ('{{$json.documentId}}','KnowledgeExtraction','FAILED','{{$json.correlationId}}'); SELECT '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: Extraction→HUMAN_REVIEW
nodes.notes: §154 Extraction → HUMAN_REVIEW.

nodes.name: SW-009/010/011 Evidence+Cite+Validate
nodes.notes: Pure: evidence (§32), citations R-06, validation R-05.

parameters.query: UPDATE repository.documents SET status='HUMAN_REVIEW' WHERE id='{{$json.documentId}}'; INSERT INTO monitoring.processing_history (document_id,stage,status,correlation_id) VALUES ('{{$json.documentId}}','KnowledgeExtraction','FAILED','{{$json.correlationId}}'); SELECT '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: NoValidKU→HUMAN_REVIEW
nodes.notes: §34 no valid KU.

nodes.name: SW-013 Embeddings
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-013 Embeddings' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-013). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

nodes.name: Emit FAILED(Embeddings)
nodes.notes: R-09 guard.

nodes.name: SW-014 Repository Writer (single txn)
nodes.notes: After import, open this node and select 'SW-014 Repository Writer' from the Workflow dropdown (canonical SW-014). The value is intentionally blank — n8n assigns the real workflow ID on your instance; it cannot be embedded in the exported JSON.

nodes.name: Emit FAILED(Repository)
nodes.notes: §154 Repository → Rollback.

parameters.query: SELECT ((SELECT count(*) FROM repository.knowledge_units WHERE document_id='{{$json.documentId}}')>0) AS certified, '{{$json.correlationId}}' AS "correlationId", '{{$json.documentId}}'   AS "documentId", '{{$json.sha256}}'       AS sha256, '{{$json.filename}}'     AS filename, '{{$json.mimeType}}'     AS "mimeType", '{{$json.source}}'       AS source;

nodes.name: SW-015 Certification
nodes.notes: SW-015 §255 certification counts (DB read).

nodes.name: Emit CERTIFIED
nodes.notes: §216 summary.

nodes.name: Emit FAILED
nodes.notes: Generic failure emitter.

nodes.name: Re-attach File Binary
nodes.notes: Binary re-attachment. Postgres nodes drop item.binary; this restores the FULL binary object from Download File (preserving filename, mimeType, storage pointer) so SW-005 OCR receives it. json unchanged. Placed on main wire SW-004 -> SW-005; checkpoint side-branch untouched.

nodes.name: Guard: documentId present
nodes.notes: Fails immediately if SW-004 returned a null/invalid documentId. This defect previously propagated 8 nodes downstream and surfaced as an unrelated checkpoint error; the null is unrecoverable (R-06 keys citations on documentId), so it must fail at the source.

parameters.query:
-- ============================================================================
-- TAXONOMY PRE-FLIGHT (§438 / R-05) — for the ACTUAL CRIE schema
--
-- Runs as the FIRST step of WF-001, BEFORE OCR. Returns exactly one row:
--     { taxonomyOk: boolean, drift: text }
--
-- SCHEMA (verified, not assumed):
--   configuration.prompt_versions (prompt_id, version TEXT, system_prompt,
--                                  user_prompt, schema, model_settings)
--   configuration.knowledge_categories (category TEXT PRIMARY KEY)
--   repository.knowledge_units CHECK chk_knowledge_units_category  (migration 0018)
--
-- There is NO `configuration.prompts` table. Do not reference one.
--
-- CRITICAL: the prompt row is selected with the SAME
--     WHERE prompt_id='PR-001' ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1
-- that SW-008's loader uses. If the preflight selected a different row than the
-- runtime, it could pass while SW-008 still loaded a drifted prompt. This mirrors
-- the loader exactly so the two can never disagree.
--
-- WHY IT EXISTS: category drift was invisible until SW-014 — after Azure OCR,
-- GPT-4o extraction, chunking and embeddings had all completed and been paid for.
-- Detection now costs one SELECT.
-- ============================================================================
WITH
-- (1) The enforcement point: the live CHECK constraint. Parsed from the catalog,
--     so it reflects the DATABASE AS DEPLOYED, not a migration file on disk.
db_constraint AS (
    SELECT ARRAY(
        SELECT DISTINCT btrim(m[1])
        FROM pg_constraint c,
             LATERAL regexp_matches(pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid), '''([^'']+)''', 'g') AS m
        WHERE c.conrelid = 'repository.knowledge_units'::regclass
          AND c.conname  = 'chk_knowledge_units_category'
        ORDER BY 1
    ) AS cats
),

-- (2) The seeded reference table. Column is `category` (TEXT PRIMARY KEY).
db_seed AS (
    SELECT COALESCE(
        (SELECT array_agg(category ORDER BY category)
           FROM configuration.knowledge_categories),
        ARRAY[]::text[]
    ) AS cats
),

-- (3) The prompt SW-008 WILL ACTUALLY LOAD. Same selection as the loader.
--     Both system_prompt and user_prompt are checked: the category list could be
--     placed in either, and concatenating avoids a false negative.
active_prompt AS (
    SELECT version,
           coalesce(system_prompt, '') || E'\n' || coalesce(user_prompt, '') AS body
      FROM configuration.prompt_versions
     WHERE prompt_id = 'PR-001'
     ORDER BY version DESC
     LIMIT 1
),

analysis AS (
    SELECT
        (SELECT cats    FROM db_constraint) AS constraint_cats,
        (SELECT cats    FROM db_seed)       AS seed_cats,
        (SELECT version FROM active_prompt) AS prompt_version,
        (SELECT body    FROM active_prompt) AS prompt_body,

        -- canonical values the active prompt never mentions
        ARRAY(
            SELECT c
              FROM unnest((SELECT cats FROM db_constraint)) AS c
             WHERE position(c IN coalesce((SELECT body FROM active_prompt), '')) = 0
        ) AS missing_from_prompt,

        -- retired §415/§504 values the prompt still PRESCRIBES.
        -- The corrected prompt NAMES these in a "Do NOT use" block, so a bare
        -- substring match would false-positive. Only flag them when that block
        -- is absent — i.e. when the prompt is telling the model TO use them.
        ARRAY(
            SELECT b
              FROM unnest(ARRAY[
                   'Capability','BusinessRule','DataModel','Performance',
                   'Compliance','Pricing','Support','Deployment','Roadmap','Other'
              ]) AS b
             WHERE position(b IN coalesce((SELECT body FROM active_prompt), '')) > 0
               AND position('Do NOT use' IN coalesce((SELECT body FROM active_prompt), '')) = 0
        ) AS superseded_in_prompt
)

SELECT
    (
            a.prompt_version IS NOT NULL                       -- PR-001 exists
        AND cardinality(a.constraint_cats) = 16                -- §438 = exactly 16
        AND a.constraint_cats @> a.seed_cats                   -- constraint ⊇ seed
        AND a.seed_cats       @> a.constraint_cats             -- seed ⊇ constraint
        AND cardinality(a.missing_from_prompt)   = 0           -- prompt covers all 16
        AND cardinality(a.superseded_in_prompt)  = 0           -- prompt prescribes none retired
    ) AS "taxonomyOk",

    nullif(concat_ws(' | ',
        CASE WHEN a.prompt_version IS NULL
             THEN 'PR-001 not found in configuration.prompt_versions' END,
        CASE WHEN cardinality(a.constraint_cats) <> 16
             THEN 'chk_knowledge_units_category holds '
                  || cardinality(a.constraint_cats)
                  || ' values; §438 defines exactly 16' END,
        CASE WHEN NOT (a.constraint_cats @> a.seed_cats AND a.seed_cats @> a.constraint_cats)
             THEN 'CHECK constraint and configuration.knowledge_categories DISAGREE' END,
        CASE WHEN cardinality(a.missing_from_prompt) > 0
             THEN 'PR-001 v' || coalesce(a.prompt_version, '?')
                  || ' (the row SW-008 loads) never mentions these canonical §438 categories: '
                  || array_to_string(a.missing_from_prompt, ', ') END,
        CASE WHEN cardinality(a.superseded_in_prompt) > 0
             THEN 'PR-001 v' || coalesce(a.prompt_version, '?')
                  || ' still prescribes RETIRED (§415/§504) categories: '
                  || array_to_string(a.superseded_in_prompt, ', ')
                  || ' — R-05 superseded these; the canonical enum is §438' END
    ), '') AS drift
FROM analysis a;

nodes.name: Preflight: taxonomy (§438/R-05)
nodes.notes: Compares the LIVE CHECK constraint (pg_get_constraintdef), configuration.knowledge_categories (column: category), and the PR-001 row that SW-008 WILL ACTUALLY LOAD from configuration.prompt_versions — selected with the SAME 'WHERE prompt_id=''PR-001'' ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1' as SW-008's loader, so the preflight and the runtime can never disagree. There is NO configuration.prompts table in this schema. Runs BEFORE OCR: the taxonomy drift that broke SW-014 was otherwise undetectable until after Azure OCR, GPT-4o extraction, chunking and embeddings had all been paid for. Terminating side-branch (Postgres drops binary, Guidelines §4).

nodes.name: Assert: taxonomy OK
nodes.notes: TERMINATING side-branch. Postgres nodes REPLACE the item and DROP binary (Guidelines §4), so the preflight must NOT sit in the main stream — Init reads the file bytes via getBinaryDataBuffer and would throw. Download File fans out to BOTH this branch and Init; Init gates on the result by node reference (§3.5.1, current-run read).
